themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to housing, healthcare, and social services. This theme has gained traction in Spain, Italy, and France, with parties like Vox, Fratelli d'Italia, and Rassemblement National advocating for these measures as a cornerstone of their political platforms.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox continues to campaign on 'national priority' policies, criticizing regional governments for failing to implement them and blaming immigrants for straining public services.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties emphasize national sovereignty and prioritize Italian citizens in social policies, often clashing with EU directives.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN promotes 'national priority' in France, advocating for stricter immigration controls and prioritizing French citizens in welfare and housing.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS supports policies that prioritize Polish citizens, particularly in social welfare and housing, aligning with broader right-wing trends.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK advocates for policies that prioritize British citizens, particularly in housing and social services, mirroring continental far-right rhetoric.

themes.title: Hungary’s political transition dominates coverage
themes.blurb: The political transition in Hungary, marked by the end of Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule and the rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party, has become a dominant cross-national theme. This shift is framed as a historic moment for Hungary and a potential model for dismantling autocratic regimes, resonating with right-wing and far-right audiences across Europe and beyond.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Coverage focuses on the transition from Orbán’s rule to Magyar’s leadership, with Magyar positioning himself as a reformer aiming to restore democracy and EU relations.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media highlights Hungary’s transition as a potential model for combating corruption and autocracy, drawing parallels to Trump’s anti-establishment rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish right-wing media discusses Hungary’s transition in the context of regional political shifts, emphasizing the importance of strong bilateral relations.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties frame Hungary’s transition as a cautionary tale about the dangers of autocracy and the need for democratic renewal.

countries.name: 🇸🇰 General (Slovakia)
countries.note: Slovak media highlights the potential for improved Hungary-Slovakia relations under Magyar’s leadership, contrasting with Orbán’s pro-Russia stance.

themes.title: Reform UK’s electoral surge reshapes UK politics
themes.blurb: Reform UK’s unprecedented gains in the UK’s local elections have dominated right-wing and far-right coverage, signaling a seismic shift in British politics. The party’s success is framed as a rejection of the traditional two-party system and a harbinger of future electoral upheaval, with Nigel Farage emerging as a central figure in this narrative.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s electoral gains are the central focus, with the party winning control of multiple councils and positioning itself as a major political force.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media highlights Reform UK’s success as evidence of a global right-wing resurgence, drawing parallels to Trump’s political movement.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties discuss Reform UK’s rise as part of a broader trend of far-right parties gaining ground in Europe.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French far-right media frames Reform UK’s success as a model for RN’s own electoral ambitions, emphasizing the potential for far-right parties to disrupt traditional politics.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Spanish far-right media covers Reform UK’s gains as part of a transnational narrative of right-wing parties challenging established political systems.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump’s policies and political influence continue to dominate right-wing and far-right media coverage worldwide. From his administration’s stance on immigration and tariffs to his potential 2028 presidential run, Trump’s actions and rhetoric are a recurring theme in right-wing narratives across the U.S., Europe, and beyond.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s policies, including tariffs, immigration, and foreign relations, are central to U.S. right-wing media coverage, shaping domestic and international narratives.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties discuss Trump’s policies in the context of U.S.-Italy relations, particularly regarding NATO, tariffs, and the Iran conflict.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish right-wing media covers Trump’s policies as part of a broader narrative about U.S.-Poland relations and the global right-wing movement.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s leadership and policies are often compared to Trump’s political movement, with Nigel Farage positioning himself as a Trump-like figure in UK politics.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Argentinian right-wing media draws parallels between Trump’s policies and those of President Javier Milei, particularly in economic and foreign policy.

themes.title: Corruption scandals rock far-right governments
themes.blurb: Corruption scandals involving high-profile figures in far-right governments have become a recurring theme in right-wing and far-right media. These scandals are often framed as politically motivated attacks on right-wing leaders, but they also highlight internal divisions and public distrust in these governments.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: The scandal involving Manuel Adorni, Chief of Staff to President Javier Milei, dominates coverage, with allegations of illicit enrichment and undeclared assets sparking political turmoil.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungarian media covers corruption allegations against former Fidesz officials, with the new Tisza government positioning itself as a reformist force aiming to address past abuses.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish right-wing media discusses corruption scandals involving PiS officials, framing them as politically motivated attacks by opposition parties.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Spanish far-right media covers corruption allegations against Vox officials, often dismissing them as smear campaigns by left-wing opponents.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media highlights corruption allegations against Trump administration officials, framing them as part of a broader effort to undermine Trump’s political movement.
